We have walked through 40+ EPC and turnkey businesses. Here is what we keep seeing. | Site reality versus office reality | Material reaches site on Tuesday. The GRN reaches Finance on Friday. By Monday, the PM has already raised an indent for the same item — because nobody told him it arrived. | Every project looks profitable | until it isn't | You bid at 18% margin. Every week, you make 5 decisions on data you do not fully trust. | Which project to push this week | You go by the loudest client call. The real signal — margin slippage, milestone delay, RA bill stuck — is buried in three spreadsheets. | How much material to release to site | You release based on the PM's WhatsApp request. You find out a month later that 22% of it is sitting unused at another site. | Which subcontractor to clear payment for | Your engineer says "work is done." His measurement sheet says one thing. The BOQ says another. You pay to keep the site moving and reconcile later. | Whether to bid for the next tender | Your team says you have bandwidth. Your existing projects say otherwise. You bid anyway — and stretch the same project managers across three more sites. | Which line item is actually bleeding | You know the project is over budget. You do not know if it is steel, labour, hire charges, or rework — until the final cost sheet, six weeks too late.
